purpose: Reviewer-oriented external-audit readiness pack. Navigation is conclusion -> ordered evidence (AuditWeave arXiv:2607.09682 lesson (b)): a reviewer starts from a verdict and walks to the evidence in order, instead of grepping. Front-loaded skeleton (GO_OWNER_PB_ROADMAP_FRONTLOAD_20260716 F5): the slots are laid out NOW; each release delta (3.4.0/3.5.0/3.6.0) drops its evidence into its named slot rather than assembling a pack from scratch at the end.
honest_status: PREPARATION, not an audit. No independent audit has occurred. See docs/AUDIT_READINESS.md and PROGRESS.md. Every artifact below is this project's OWN instrument — that is exactly the boundary an external review is for.

conclusions.statement: A verified receipt is tamper-EVIDENT and, when anchored, tamper-RESISTANT: the chain head is bound to an external append-only reference so a storage-controlling actor cannot silently recompute it.
conclusions.closes_external_open_problem: AuditWeave open problem (a): 'tamper-evident, not resistant — actors controlling storage could recompute chains'.

evidence_ordered.note: the resistance story, stated before the auditor asks

OTS calendar-independence: an upgraded proof verifies offline against a relying-party Bitcoin header; donation-financed calendars affect stamping availability, not verifiability; RFC 3161 is the immediate legal second anchor (WP-E)
OpenTimestamps (Bitcoin) external anchor verify + calendar transparency (calendar_uris / operators)
self-contained evidence pack: build + OFFLINE verify (no socket), the calendar-independent verify path
RFC 3161 TSA external anchor verify (the eIDAS-recognized legal second anchor)
calendar outage / collusion+backdating / pending-never-pass / operator redundancy, plus the ripemd160-free confirmed-path fixture
F1 corpus incl. forged-anchor-own-frozen negative vector

conclusions.statement: verify correctly separates an authentic, unmodified receipt from a tampered or forged one, and never raw-crashes on hostile input.

evidence_ordered.note: F1 offline corpus, 29 cases, one common vocabulary
F1 the one comparator all three differential layers share
F4 property-based JSON type-confusion matrix over the AST-discovered verify_* set — never-raise, structural
Hypothesis fuzz of the byte/string parser class

conclusions.statement: The relation-lineage aggregation ladder is a sound join (one FAIL poisons the chain; an unresolved edge is never upgraded), and lineage never raises cryptoValid.

evidence_ordered.note: F3 versioned model, obligations O1-O4 proven (bounded enumeration + z3-extended), grounded against the implementation
honest scope: logic yes; primitives/side-channels no

conclusions.statement: Every published artifact is built hermetically, is byte-reproducible, and carries build provenance separated from the build (SLSA L3 shape).

evidence_ordered.note: F2 hermetic cleanroom + determinism (two sdists byte-identical)
F2 deterministic sdist normaliser (SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH + canonical tar)
F2 SLSA-L3 reusable signing workflow (signature separated from build) — built + CI-exercised (dry_run); release.yml adoption pending, see docs/roadmap/FRONTLOAD.md

delivers: F2 hermetic gate + reproducible sdist + reusable attest workflow
